# OnePWS Master AI Chatbot Knowledge Base

This master Markdown file consolidates all Q&A from the earlier OnePWS knowledge-base files and adds the newly uploaded catalogs: Defense Consoles, Architectural/Fit-Out Solutions, Modular Operation Theatre, XLAT, and XLAT SE. Use it as a direct AI chatbot training source.

## Training Rules

- Answer in English unless the user asks otherwise.
- Prefer confirmed OnePWS facts from this file.
- If exact pricing, warranty, lead time, or project feasibility is requested, collect project details and escalate to the OnePWS team.
- Do not invent unconfirmed specifications.
- For year-wise company milestones, use the authoritative website timeline section below. It replaces older truncated or conflicting timeline wording.

## Included Source Families

- Website screenshots and supplied timeline

- Corporate Interior Solutions catalog

- ATC Consoles catalog

- Auditorium Solutions catalog

- Control Room Solutions catalog

- Control Desk Solutions catalog

- Defense Consoles catalog

- Architectural / Fit-Out Solutions catalog

- Modular Operation Theatre catalog

- XLAT product catalog

- XLAT SE product catalog


# Authoritative Website Timeline - Updated Milestones

Use this section as the final source of truth for year-wise OnePWS/Pyrotech Workspace Solutions milestones in the chatbot training data.

## 2006

In 2006, Pyrotech Workspace Solutions Pvt. Ltd. was founded by Mr. Puneet Talesara, an IIT Guwahati alumnus, with a vision to provide world-class Control Rooms and Control Desk Solutions.

## 2007

In 2007, the company introduced an innovative line of modular control room consoles to the Indian market, redefining efficiency and flexibility. These customizable solutions elevated operational standards and empowered operators with comfort and precision in critical tasks.

## 2009

In 2009, continuing the PWS success story, the company established a cutting-edge manufacturing plant spanning 125,000 sq. ft., strengthening its commitment to excellence and innovation in production.

## 2011

In 2011, the company installed an ultra-modern metal processing plant, using the latest technologies to elevate manufacturing capabilities. This advancement reinforced its dedication to efficiency, precision, and industry-leading innovation.

## 2012

In 2012, the company delivered its first-ever control room outside India. This was a major stepping stone that brought new challenges, learning, opportunities, and access to new markets.

## 2013

In 2013, the company inaugurated a dedicated 10,000 sq. ft. showroom exclusively showcasing cutting-edge control room consoles and interiors. The showroom became a hub for awareness and firsthand experience of OnePWS solutions.

## 2014

In 2014, the company achieved a groundbreaking milestone by becoming the world’s first company to attain Greenguard Gold Certification for consoles. This highlighted its commitment to healthier control room environments and set a new benchmark for the industry.

## 2015

In 2015, designing and delivering control rooms for the Defence & Aerospace sector became a proud milestone for Pyrotech Workspace Solutions. After three months of dedicated effort, the company secured its first order in this sector, fulfilling its aspiration to serve the nation.

## 2016

In 2016, the company hosted Confes’16 – The Control Room Fest, one of the largest events in the Control Room Interiors & Console Industry. The event brought together 180 delegates from 5 countries, 75 companies, and 10 industries, encouraging collaboration across sectors.

## 2017

In 2017, Pyrotech Workspace Solutions achieved 15% year-on-year growth and emerged as India’s market leader in turnkey control room solutions within just nine years. By this point, it had delivered 40,000+ operator consoles and 180+ design-build control rooms across 20+ countries.

## 2018

In 2018, the company became the first and sole company globally to integrate SAP into the console industry. This implementation strengthened data management, process reliability, innovation, and customer confidence.

## 2019

In 2019, the company successfully completed India’s biggest incubation center project: an eight-floor building with a total floor area of 100,000 sq. ft., completed within a record time span of five months.

## 2020

By 2020, over 18 years, the company had grown into a skilled team of 400 professionals with a proven track record of 450+ control rooms delivered, 60,000+ desks installed, presence in 25+ countries, and 100+ appreciation letters. Driven by passion, the company consistently delivered “Beyond the Obvious” results on a global scale.

## 2021

In 2021, the company advanced its journey of excellence with key achievements including the State Safety Award for its commitment to safety and the UCCI Excellence Award for industry leadership. With a turnover of ₹125 Cr, it reinforced its strong market position and consistent growth.

## 2022

In 2022, the company completed the Chandigarh Smart City Control Room and launched its new vertical, Admin Interior. It also delivered advanced facilities such as the Center of Revenue and Research Department and the Rajiv Gandhi Center of Advanced Technology, reflecting its drive for innovation and excellence.

## 2023

In 2023, the company received the Export Excellence Award from FIEO and expanded its footprint by supplying advanced console solutions to IGI Airport, New Delhi. These milestones reflected its commitment to quality and international growth.

## 2024

In 2024, the prestigious alumni award recognized outstanding career achievements and impactful contributions across fields such as business, innovation, leadership, and public service. It celebrated individuals who brought pride to the institution and uplifted lives through their work, including Mr. Puneet Talesara’s distinguished alumni recognition.

## 2025

In 2025, the company launched PWS FLOOR, a high-performance Raised Access Floor (RAF) tile solution engineered for mission-critical environments. Introduced under the trusted legacy of ONEPWS, PWS FLOOR reflects the company’s commitment to quality, innovation, and reliability in advanced infrastructure solutions for control rooms and high-performance workspaces.

## 2026

As of 5 February 2026, all specialized capabilities were unified under one name: OnePWS. The transition represents more than a name change; it reflects a strategic evolution and strengthens the company’s ability to operate as a single, intelligent ecosystem delivering integrated environments at scale.

## Master Questions and Answers

### Q1. What is the company name?

**Answer:** The company name is OnePWS Pvt. Ltd.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q2. What was OnePWS formerly known as?

**Answer:** OnePWS was formerly known as Pyrotech Workspace Solutions Pvt. Ltd.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q3. What is the OnePWS tagline?

**Answer:** The OnePWS tagline used across catalogs is "Everything else looks obsolete" or "Everything else becomes obsolete".

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q4. Who founded OnePWS?

**Answer:** OnePWS was founded by Mr. Puneet Talesara.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q5. Who is Puneet Talesara?

**Answer:** Puneet Talesara is the Founder, Managing Director/CEO, entrepreneur, product designer, inventor, and an IIT Guwahati alumnus associated with OnePWS.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q6. Where is OnePWS headquartered?

**Answer:** OnePWS is headquartered at Plot No. B-438, Road No. 18A, Bhamashah Industrial Area, Kaladwas, Udaipur, Rajasthan 313003, India. Some catalogs mention Udaipur, Rajasthan 313002.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q7. What does OnePWS specialize in?

**Answer:** OnePWS specializes in design-to-build control room solutions, control room consoles, ATC consoles, auditorium solutions, corporate interior solutions, command and control centers, and mission-critical workspace infrastructure.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q8. What is the company vision?

**Answer:** The vision is to give a Beyond the Obvious Experience through passion and innovation, bringing pride and trust to stakeholders while adding value to society.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q9. What is the company mission?

**Answer:** The mission is to become the most admired global brand in its class, continually offer timeless and innovative products, and lead through research, operational excellence, determined people, processes, and best-in-class technology.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q10. What is the OnePWS value proposition?

**Answer:** OnePWS provides integrated design, engineering, manufacturing, installation, testing, commissioning, and lifecycle support under one roof, reducing interface risk and improving quality.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q11. What makes OnePWS different from conventional vendors?

**Answer:** Unlike conventional multi-agency execution, OnePWS offers a one-stop design-built concept with holistic design, modularity, factory-made quality, quick serviceability, precision manufacturing, and reduced execution time.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q12. How does OnePWS describe its design philosophy?

**Answer:** OnePWS says manufacturing becomes workspace when empathy, passion, and innovation come together. Its design philosophy focuses on improving lives through better, safer, ergonomic, and future-ready spaces.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q13. What does Beyond the Obvious mean for OnePWS?

**Answer:** Beyond the Obvious means creating solutions that go beyond standard expectations through innovation, safety, ergonomics, quality, and a superior user experience.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q14. How large is the manufacturing area?

**Answer:** The uploaded catalogs and screenshots mention a manufacturing area of approximately 225,000 sq. ft.; some older CEO notes refer to 2.5 lakh sq. ft.

### Q48. What entrepreneurial award is mentioned for Puneet Talesara?

**Answer:** The website screenshot mentions an Outstanding Entrepreneur Award by ISA - District 14 at Automation India Week 2015, Mumbai.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q49. Which management system certifications does OnePWS mention?

**Answer:** OnePWS mentions ISO 9001, ISO 14001, and ISO 45001 management system certifications.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q50. What is ISO 11064 relevant to?

**Answer:** ISO 11064 is relevant to ergonomic design and control center/control room standards. OnePWS states that its designs adhere to international ergonomic norms ISO 11064.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q51. What is Greenguard certification?

**Answer:** Greenguard certification indicates low chemical emissions of VOCs, helping create a healthier indoor environment.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q52. What is Greenguard Gold certification?

**Answer:** Greenguard Gold is a more stringent version of Greenguard certification with additional health-based criteria for chemicals.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q53. What is EPD certification?

**Answer:** EPD certification supports credible green product claims by documenting environmental impact information.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q54. What does LEVEL certification indicate?

**Answer:** The LEVEL mark confirms compliance with ANSI/BIFMA e3-2019, assessing environmental, health, and social impacts of furniture products.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q55. What is BIFMA X5.5 certification?

**Answer:** BIFMA X5.5 focuses on safety and stringent performance standards for console systems used in 24/7 applications.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q56. What does RoHS certification indicate?

**Answer:** RoHS indicates restriction of hazardous substances and confirms that the product avoids substances restricted by European Union directives.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q57. What fire safety testing is mentioned?

**Answer:** Control room and auditorium finishes are tested for surface spread of flame and lower smoke emission to reduce hazardous accident risks.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q58. What seismic certification is mentioned?

**Answer:** Console and interior finishing items such as wall paneling, ceiling, and partitions are tested and certified for Seismic Zone 5 vibrations as per IS 1893 Part 1.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q59. What UL safety testing is mentioned?

**Answer:** OnePWS states that the product has been tested by UL to nationally recognized safety standards and found free from reasonably foreseeable fire, electric shock, and related hazards in Division 2/Zone environments.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q60. What FIRA certifications are mentioned?

**Answer:** PWS consoles are FIRA International certified for BS EN ISO 11064:2013 standards for control centers and BS EN 527:2011 standards for fixed-height and fully height-adjustable desking.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q66. How should the chatbot answer pricing questions?

**Answer:** The chatbot should explain that pricing depends on scope, site, seating count, materials, technology integration, customization, compliance needs, and installation requirements, then ask for project details and offer to connect the user with the OnePWS team.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q67. How should the chatbot answer lead time questions?

**Answer:** The chatbot should say lead time depends on project size, engineering approval, manufacturing scope, site readiness, and installation complexity. It should collect project details and escalate to the sales/project team for an accurate schedule.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q68. How should the chatbot answer warranty questions?

**Answer:** The chatbot should explain that warranty and AMC are part of OnePWS execution and lifecycle support, but exact terms should be confirmed project-wise with the OnePWS team.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q69. How should the chatbot handle questions outside the knowledge base?

**Answer:** The chatbot should politely say it does not have confirmed information and should offer to connect the user with OnePWS for accurate details.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q70. How should the chatbot answer competitor comparison questions?

**Answer:** The chatbot should focus on OnePWS strengths: integrated design-build delivery, in-house manufacturing, ergonomics, certifications, SAP-enabled project data, modular quality, and lifecycle support, without making unverified negative claims about competitors.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q71. How should the chatbot collect leads?

**Answer:** The chatbot should ask for name, company, phone, email, city, project type, approximate size or seating capacity, timeline, and any specific requirements.

**Source family:** 01_company_brand_contacts.md

### Q72. What are OnePWS control room solutions?

**Answer:** OnePWS control room solutions are integrated design-build solutions for mission-critical command and control centers, including consoles, interiors, ergonomics, acoustic planning, illumination, traffic flow, and technology integration.

**Source family:** 02_control_room_solutions.md

### Q73. What does the control room design-built solution include?

**Answer:** The execution process includes project planning, manufacturing, quality control, factory acceptance test (FAT), packing and forwarding, site acceptance test (SAT), warranty, and AMC.

### Q75. What are the benefits of 100% modular interiors and console solutions?

### Q76. How does OnePWS handle ergonomic studies?

**Answer:** OnePWS evaluates custom console designs against ergonomic norms, including knee space, foot space, worktop height, visual sightlines, viewing angles, grip reach, and general lux levels.

**Source family:** 02_control_room_solutions.md

### Q77. What is traffic route study in control rooms?

**Answer:** Traffic route study maps operator movement in normal, emergency, startup, and maintenance modes to ensure safe, functional console arrangement without disrupting operations.

**Source family:** 02_control_room_solutions.md

### Q78. What is human factor engineering in control rooms?

**Answer:** Human factor engineering applies physical and psychological user characteristics to control room interior design so operators can work safely, efficiently, and comfortably.

**Source family:** 02_control_room_solutions.md

### Q79. Why is acoustics important in a control room?

**Answer:** Acoustics matter because excessive noise reduces operator performance, while overly quiet rooms can be uncomfortable. OnePWS designs balance background sound and noise control.

**Source family:** 02_control_room_solutions.md

### Q80. Why is illumination important in a control room?

**Answer:** Proper illumination improves operator performance, reduces fatigue, and adds life to the control room environment.

**Source family:** 02_control_room_solutions.md

### Q100. What are OnePWS ATC consoles?

**Answer:** OnePWS ATC consoles are mission-critical air traffic control workstations designed for towers, approach centers, training simulators, and related air traffic management environments.

**Source family:** 03_atc_consoles.md

### Q101. What is the purpose of ATC consoles?

**Answer:** ATC consoles support air traffic controllers with ergonomic layouts, precision engineering, technology integration, uninterrupted operations, regulatory compliance, and situational awareness.

**Source family:** 03_atc_consoles.md

### Q102. What ATC environments does OnePWS serve?

**Answer:** OnePWS serves ATC towers, approach centers, training simulators, supervisory monitoring zones, area control rooms, and other airport command environments.

**Source family:** 03_atc_consoles.md

### Q103. What are key ATC console features?

**Answer:** Key features include unobstructed sightlines, scalable mission-specific configurations, compact high-efficiency layouts, concealed wiring for safety, adjustable components, ISO 11064 and HFDS compliance, and integrated foot switches for hands-free control.

**Source family:** 03_atc_consoles.md

### Q104. What is a Tower Console?

**Answer:** A Tower Console is designed for 24/7 visibility and provides optimized sightlines for unobstructed runway and airspace monitoring with display mounts and integrated communication interfaces.

**Source family:** 03_atc_consoles.md

### Q105. What is a Supervisory Console in ATC?

**Answer:** A Supervisory Console supports centralized monitoring by integrating multi-level control frameworks, assigning roles, overseeing real-time data, managing alerts, and coordinating operations.

**Source family:** 03_atc_consoles.md

### Q106. What is an Area Console?

**Answer:** An Area Console is designed for high-fidelity training and operations, replicating live ATC operations and giving trainees real-world scenario handling and decision-making experience.

**Source family:** 03_atc_consoles.md

### Q107. What is an Approach Console?

**Answer:** An Approach Console helps manage aircraft transitioning between en-route and terminal airspace, integrating radar feeds and secure communication systems.

**Source family:** 03_atc_consoles.md

### Q108. Why are sightlines important in ATC consoles?

**Answer:** Sightlines are critical because controllers must maintain clear visual awareness of runway, aircraft, displays, and surrounding airspace.

**Source family:** 03_atc_consoles.md

### Q109. Why is ergonomic design important for ATC?

**Answer:** ATC operations are continuous and high-pressure, so ergonomics reduces fatigue, supports accuracy, and helps controllers work safely during long shifts.

**Source family:** 03_atc_consoles.md

### Q110. What does OnePWS mean by regulatory compliance in ATC?

**Answer:** It means designing consoles and layouts in alignment with relevant standards, ergonomic norms, operational safety needs, and mission-critical aviation requirements.

**Source family:** 03_atc_consoles.md

**Answer:** A soft ceiling uses acoustic padding behind a textile layer and helps condition sound across a frequency range such as 125 to 5000 Hz.

### Q144. What is the role of acoustic ceiling panels?

**Answer:** Acoustic ceiling panels provide high absorption, especially for low-frequency content in halls using electroacoustic sound reinforcement.

### Q145. How does hall shape affect sound?

**Answer:** A hall shape can help reflect sound effectively throughout the space, improving intelligibility and distribution.

**Answer:** Lightweight speakers distributed overhead or across the venue improve sound amplification and uniform coverage.

**Answer:** Stage flooring supports high loads, continuous movement, equipment use, anti-skid safety, vibration control, and acoustic clarity.

### Q1177. What are OnePWS Defense Consoles?

**Answer:** OnePWS Defense Consoles are mission-ready MIL-grade console and workstation solutions for defense command, control, communication, monitoring, surveillance, tactical operations, and high-security mission-critical environments.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1178. What does Mission-Ready MIL Grid Consoles mean?

**Answer:** Mission-Ready MIL Grid Consoles refers to console systems built for modern battlespace operations where rugged durability, secure communication, high operator efficiency, modular design, and mission continuity are required.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1179. Which defense environments are OnePWS consoles designed for?

**Answer:** They are designed for navy, ground, airborne, submarine, command center, operations room, monitoring room, simulation, training, and tactical control environments.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1180. What operational console types are shown for armed forces?

**Answer:** The defense catalog shows Navy Consoles, Ground Consoles, and Airborne Consoles as operational console types across armed forces.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1181. What is a Navy Console?

**Answer:** A Navy Console is a marine-grade workstation engineered for maritime operations with corrosion-resistant materials, secure monitoring, mission software integration, waterproof input devices, stability, durability, and continuous operational use.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1182. What is a Submarine Console?

**Answer:** A Submarine Console is a compact, secure, operator-focused console solution for underwater and submarine operating environments where space, visibility, equipment integration, and reliability are critical.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1183. What is a Ground Console?

**Answer:** A Ground Console is a rugged and modular console for command vehicles, static command centers, tactical command rooms, and field operations where durability and adaptability are required.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1184. What is an Airborne Console?

**Answer:** An Airborne Console is a lightweight, compact, mission-specific workstation for air force and aviation environments that supports real-time mission data, ergonomic operation, and equipment integration.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1185. What are the core features of defense consoles?

**Answer:** Core features include multi-screen mission-data displays, modular and mission-specific configurations, rugged construction, secure communication support, equipment integration, ergonomic operator layouts, cable management, ventilation, and reliability for critical operations.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1186. How do defense consoles support mission-critical decisions?

**Answer:** They organize displays, communication tools, input devices, mission software, equipment cabinets, power, cooling, and cable routing so operators can maintain situational awareness and make timely decisions.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1187. What makes OnePWS defense consoles suitable for harsh environments?

**Answer:** They are designed with rugged construction, operator-centric ergonomics, durable finishes, equipment protection, controlled airflow, and mission-specific configuration options.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1188. Do defense consoles support secure communication?

**Answer:** Yes. The defense catalog positions these consoles for secure communication, command, control, and mission-critical monitoring environments.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1189. Are defense consoles modular?

**Answer:** Yes. Defense consoles are positioned as modular and configurable solutions that can be adapted for different armed-force applications and mission requirements.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1190. Can defense consoles include multiple screens?

**Answer:** Yes. The catalog shows defense consoles with multi-screen displays for real-time mission data, navigation, monitoring, and control applications.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1191. What are the ergonomic considerations for defense consoles?

**Answer:** The catalog states that custom consoles are evaluated against ergonomic norms, including knee space, foot space, worktop height, visual sight lines, viewing angles, grip reach, and general lux levels.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1192. Do defense consoles follow ISO 11064?

**Answer:** The defense catalog references ergonomic design alignment with ISO 11064 / Human Factor Design Standards for operator comfort and compliance.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1193. Which certifications are associated with defense console systems?

**Answer:** Certifications and compliance references include ISO 11064, Greenguard, Greenguard Gold, EPD, BIFMA X5.5, RoHS, LEVEL, FIRA, ASTM surface spread of flame, LCA, Seismic Zone 5, and UL Listed references.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1194. What is the global reach mentioned in the defense catalog?

**Answer:** The defense catalog states that design-built solutions have been appreciated by more than 300 customers from 30+ countries.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1195. What company statistics are repeated in the defense catalog?

**Answer:** The defense catalog mentions 2006 formation, 2009 dedicated manufacturing facility, 225,000 sq. ft. manufacturing area, 400+ control room design-built solutions, 300+ customers served globally, 250+ ergonomic studies, 60,000+ consoles delivered globally, 30+ countries, and 20+ international quality certifications.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1196. What should the chatbot say when a user asks for defense console pricing?

**Answer:** The chatbot should say that pricing depends on console type, number of operator positions, screen configuration, ruggedization, security needs, materials, installation scope, and project location, then collect project details and connect the lead with the OnePWS team.

**Source family:** Defense Consoles Catalog

### Q1197. What are OnePWS Architectural Solutions?

**Answer:** OnePWS Architectural Solutions are fit-out, ceiling, cladding, access floor, and architectural interior systems designed for commercial, corporate, control-room, healthcare, and mission-critical environments.

**Source family:** Architectural Solutions / Fit-Out Catalog

### Q1198. What are OnePWS Fit-Out solutions?

**Answer:** OnePWS Fit-Out solutions combine design, manufacturing, installation, and execution expertise to create customized interiors, workspaces, control environments, healthcare spaces, and architectural systems.

### Q1202. What is Aluminium Batten?

**Answer:** Aluminium Batten is a lightweight, strong, and durable ceiling or cladding system designed for commercial and institutional applications with modern aesthetics and easy installation.

### Q1203. Where can Aluminium Batten be used?

### Q1207. What is an access floor?

**Answer:** An access floor is a raised flooring system that creates service space below the walking surface for cables, air distribution, power, data, and utilities, especially useful in control rooms and technical spaces.

**Source family:** Architectural Solutions / Fit-Out Catalog

### Q1208. Why are access floors important in control rooms?

**Answer:** Access floors support organized cable management, equipment flexibility, easy maintenance, future upgrades, cooling/airflow needs, and clean technical infrastructure below the floor.

**Source family:** Architectural Solutions / Fit-Out Catalog

### Q1231. What products are listed in the MOT catalog?

**Answer:** Products shown include modular wall paneling system, wall graphic panels, in-built storage, peripheral ambient light, hermetic sealed ceiling system, diffuser plenum/laminar airflow, hermetic automatic sliding door, scrub sink, X-ray viewer, writing board, pressure relief damper, glass windows, hatch pass-through box, swing/lead door, surgeon control panel, and OT pendant.

### Q1232. What is a modular wall paneling system in an OT?

**Answer:** It is a modular wall system for operating theatres that supports cleanability, durability, infection control, integration of services, and controlled room performance.

**Source family:** Modular Operation Theatre Catalog

### Q1233. What is a hermetic sealed ceiling system?

**Answer:** It is a sealed ceiling system designed to support cleanroom performance, controlled airflow, hygiene, and integration of lighting and medical services.

**Source family:** Modular Operation Theatre Catalog

### Q1234. What is a diffuser plenum or laminar airflow system?

**Answer:** It is an airflow delivery system used in operating theatres to maintain controlled, clean, directed air over the surgical area.

**Source family:** Modular Operation Theatre Catalog

### Q1235. What is a hermetic automatic sliding door?

**Answer:** A hermetic automatic sliding door is a sealed OT door designed to maintain pressure, hygiene, and controlled access in sterile areas.

**Source family:** Modular Operation Theatre Catalog

### Q1236. What is a scrub sink?

**Answer:** A scrub sink is a hand-washing station for medical staff before entering sterile zones or operating theatres.

**Source family:** Modular Operation Theatre Catalog

### Q1237. What is a surgeon control panel?

**Answer:** A surgeon control panel is an integrated control interface that can manage operating-room systems such as lighting, HVAC, clocks, alarms, and other OT controls.

**Source family:** Modular Operation Theatre Catalog

### Q1238. What is an OT pendant?

**Answer:** An OT pendant is an overhead or wall-mounted equipment service system used to provide medical gases, electrical outlets, data, and equipment support near the operating table.

**Source family:** Modular Operation Theatre Catalog

### Q1239. What is OR integration in the MOT catalog?

**Answer:** OR integration connects medical devices, displays, cameras, endoscopes, monitors, technical racks, video conferencing, classrooms, auditoriums, remote participants, and recording/streaming systems.

**Source family:** Modular Operation Theatre Catalog

### Q1240. What video conferencing functions are mentioned for OR integration?

**Answer:** The catalog mentions connecting multiple sources, image and data routing, video and snapshot recording, live software streaming, PACS/HIS connectivity, secure access control, and integrated video conferencing.

**Source family:** Modular Operation Theatre Catalog

**Answer:** XLAT can support transport, energy, utilities, defense, command centers, surveillance rooms, technical workspaces, and other mission-critical environments.

### Q1247. What is the main purpose of XLAT?

**Answer:** The main purpose of XLAT is to improve operator comfort, workflow efficiency, equipment organization, monitoring capability, and adaptability in mission-critical workspaces.

### Q1248. What is certified operator comfort for XLAT?

**Answer:** Certified operator comfort means the console is designed for long-duration mission-critical use with ISO 11064 ergonomic design standards and certifications for safety, compliance, and material integrity.

### Q1249. Which certifications are shown for XLAT?

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1250. What are XLAT structured-for-success features?

**Answer:** Features include highly functional design, optimally engineered structure, integrated cabinets and storage, dual to multi-tier configuration support, ISO 11064 compliance, sustainable and maintenance-free materials, environmental friendliness, ergonomic moulded wrist pads, customizable layouts, advanced monitoring design integration, and timeless aesthetic style.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1251. What is PU-edge ergonomic worksurface in XLAT?

**Answer:** It is a worktop edge detail designed to improve operator comfort by giving a softer, ergonomic contact surface for hands and wrists during long work periods.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1252. What is XLAT modularity?

**Answer:** XLAT modularity means operators and project teams can reconfigure, expand, or adapt the console with minimal effort, supporting technology upgrades, spatial realignment, and functional changes without disrupting continuity.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1253. How does XLAT manage equipment?

**Answer:** XLAT includes organized equipment cabinets, enhanced airflow for cooling, cable organization, storage, and serviceable rear or internal access depending on configuration.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1254. Does XLAT support cooling?

**Answer:** Yes. XLAT product visuals show enhanced airflow for optimal cooling and organized equipment cabinet design.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1255. How is XLAT SE introduced in the catalog?

**Answer:** XLAT SE is introduced as a benchmark in control room console design where form, function, and future-readiness converge for critical environments requiring seamless performance and operational clarity.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1256. Which sectors can use XLAT SE?

**Answer:** XLAT SE can be used in defense, transport, utilities, emergency control, and other mission-critical control room environments.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1257. What is certified operator comfort for XLAT SE?

**Answer:** XLAT SE brings industrial design excellence and technical precision together, with components conforming to ISO 11064 ergonomics and references to RoHS, BIFMA Level 3, and ASTM material and safety standards.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1258. What are XLAT SE structured-for-success features?

**Answer:** Features include modular operational flexibility, concealed cable management, robust durable structure, ergonomic display mounting, protective edge safety, elegant certified worktops, sleek polished finishes, eco-conscious material, quick hassle-free assembly, and smart storage integration.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1259. What is Powered by Modular Intelligence in XLAT SE?

**Answer:** It means XLAT SE uses a dynamic modular structure that enables effortless adjustment and expansion for changing control-room demands while supporting new technologies and future-ready workspace functionality.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1260. Does XLAT SE support touch-control interfaces?

**Answer:** Yes. The catalog shows an embedded touch-control interface with functions such as desk height, task light, and LED indicators.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1261. Does XLAT SE support sit-to-stand use?

**Answer:** Yes. The catalog describes a motorized sit-to-stand mechanism supporting dynamic posture adjustments during long operations.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1262. How does XLAT SE handle storage and cooling?

**Answer:** The catalog shows streamlined storage solutions and directed airflow cooling mechanisms for equipment integration and thermal management.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1263. How should the chatbot answer XLAT vs XLAT SE questions?

**Answer:** It should explain that both are mission-critical control-room consoles; XLAT emphasizes flexible command-space adaptability and broad modular configuration, while XLAT SE emphasizes streamlined modular flexibility, operator-centric design, concealed cable management, smart storage, and sit-to-stand/touch-control features.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs

### Q1786. Can you explain: How should the chatbot answer XLAT vs XLAT SE questions?

**Answer:** It should explain that both are mission-critical control-room consoles; XLAT emphasizes flexible command-space adaptability and broad modular configuration, while XLAT SE emphasizes streamlined modular flexibility, operator-centric design, concealed cable management, smart storage, and sit-to-stand/touch-control features.

**Source family:** XLAT and XLAT SE Product Catalogs - expanded phrasing

Ground Applications
Consoles for Army use offer rugged modularity, allowing seamless
integration into field-deployable systems, armored vehicles, and
static command centers. Designed with adjustable brightness,
wide-angle viewing, and custom-configurable control panels, they
provide battlefield flexibility, secure communication, and rapid
operator adaptability across changing terrain and mission types.

--- PAGE 9 ---
Engineered for Effortless
Transitions
The XLAT SE console incorporates a motorized sit-to-stand
mechanism designed to support dynamic posture adjustments
during extended operational shifts. The height-adjustable
system enables seamless transition between seated and Sit-Stand Mechanism
standing positions, reducing musculoskeletal strain and
enhancing operator focus. Integrated with programmable
presets and smart touch control, the mechanism ensures
precise height modulation tailored to individual
anthropometric requirements.
Design Pending

--- PAGE 10 ---
ie “ —

y > \ aus
Ergonomic Superiority
XLAT SE adheres to ISO 11064 and HFDS ergonomic standards, ensuring operational efficiency
and user well-being. The design is based on validated anthropometric data and can be adapted to
meet country-specific ergonomic guidelines, accommodating diverse regional user profiles. This
approach promotes optimal posture, enhances visual comfort, and reduces operator fatigue
during extended operational periods.

= XLAT
XLAT represents a control room solution crafted to give the end-user complete command
of their operational space. Engineered to accommodate radical shifts in technology, it
delivers a modular framework tailored to streamline workflows and improve process
efficiency. With intelligent system integration, XLAT responds dynamically to the evolving
demands of mission-critical environments.
Designed for operators seeking adaptability and ergonomic superiority, XLAT provides
limitless configuration possibilities. From modular desk layouts to advanced monitoring

<= integration, every aspect of the XLAT range caters to diverse spatial and functional

requirements, making it a preferred choice across varied domains including transport,
energy, utilities, and defense.
